paper.title: Rep Smarter, Not Harder: AI Hypertrophy Coaching with Wearable Sensors and Edge Neural Networks
paper.abstract: Optimizing resistance training for hypertrophy requires balancing proximity to muscular failure, often quantified by Repetitions in Reserve (RiR), with fatigue management. However, subjective RiR assessment is unreliable, leading to suboptimal training stimuli or excessive fatigue. This paper introduces a novel system for real-time feedback on near-failure states (RiR $\le$ 2) during resistance exercise using only a single wrist-mounted Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU). We propose a two-stage pip

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Using a two-stage pipeline with a ResNet-based model for segmentation and an LSTM for classification can achieve high accuracy in real-time feedback for resistance training
technical_details: Raspberry Pi 5 and iPhone 16 for testing
112 ms average inference latency on Raspberry Pi 5
23.5 ms average inference latency on iPhone 16
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, the system can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices due to its low latency and feasibility for edge computation

review_questions: Can you find specific memory numbers in the paper?
